maps__projects_none_with_n_organisations: All projects for {n} organisations
slow_lower_wallaby_offer: Points are grouped together in Layers. You can toggle their visibility in the Layers section below.
alert_suave_moth_glow: Each layer is part of a Project and offered by an Organisation. If you are interested in a specific one, select them in the Organisations and Projects sections below. The Layer section will update to show what you've filtered for.
slow_quiet_dolphin_seek: Tip: Breadcrumbs above a layer name show you the project and organisation it belongs to.
bad_honest_pigeon_borrow: Use filters to only show points you are interested in. Select Neighbourhoods or filter by any of the Categories available in the layers.
watery_cuddly_niklas_harbor: You can search for region, district, or neighbourhood name. The number shows you how many places are in that neighbourhood for your active layers.
arable_mild_snake_pick: Layers add collections of points to the map. Filters select points from those layers, and hide the rest.
drab_many_gibbon_fulfill: If you select a neighbourhood from search, this highlights and focuses points in the neighbourhood. Filters only remove points that don't match your filters.
such_tense_samuel_smile: Use Stars to track the places you want to visit and have visited.
neat_watery_wasp_buy: Tap ☆ Star to add it to your list. Tap it again to remove it. Every place card has this button in the bottom left corner.
same_lower_orangutan_enchant: Tap ✓ Check In to record your visit. Every place card has this button in the bottom left corner.
extra_yummy_midge_wish: We use Google to authenticate you. Your name, profile picture, and password are managed through Google, and cannot be changed here.
petty_steep_dove_clasp: The app is currently available in English, Traditional, and Simplified Chinese.
sleek_main_shell_taste: We would love to acknowledge your contributions to the map. Please provide the name to be credited. This can either be a nickname, your legal name, or an organisation.
trite_key_insect_tickle: When you open the app, the map will start off with the default Layers you've enabled.
big_awake_grebe_seek: Preview new features before they are released.
settings_language_auto_translate_descrip: If content is unavailable in your language(s), automatically translate it to your preferred language.
settings_language_show_translate_button_: Shows a button to switch between the translated and original descriptions
settings_language_prefer_placeholders_de: Shows placeholder text like "Unknown" instead of machine-translating content that's unavailable in your language(s)

report_missing__curatorial_review: A curator will review your report
report_missing__initial_instruction: Let them know why it's missing
report_missing__add_photo_instructions: Add a photo to help verify your report
weird_great_barbel_gaze: Other than rejecting the report, there are three actions you can take to affect a feature's state:
tired_quick_snake_enjoy: Feature will no longer be shown on the public map, AND it will be removed from the Admin panel. Take this action if visiting this feature is no longer of public interest, as it has physically been removed. In effect, this unpublishes the feature, and makes it unavailable in the admin panel.
great_sad_rook_cure: Feature will no longer be shown on the public map, BUT it will still be visible in the Admin panel. Take this action, either because (1) visiting this feature is (temporarily) not possible; or (2) the maintainer wishes to keep the feature in the collection, but remove it from the public map. In effect, this sets the feature as inaccessible and unpublished.
flat_direct_bobcat_praise: Feature should be intangibalised only if its presence on the public map can provide practical, historic context for the feature — places where e.g. historic events took place; there's a striking contrast with the present; or the locality is more salient than the missing materiality of the feature. In effect, this marks a feature as intangible, but neither removes it from the public map, nor from the Admin panel.
strong_next_zebra_quiz: Would you like to accept or reject this user-submitted Feature?
awful_real_newt_lift: The feature will no longer appear anywhere.
careful_inner_grebe_skip: You will be redirected to the feature detail page where you can edit the user-submitted information, add translations, set the address, and further polish the Feature before it is published on the public map.
few_sunny_wren_push: Would you like to accept or reject this user-submitted photo to the Feature's gallery?
great_loose_gull_assure: None of the submitted photos will appear anywhere.
mild_deft_larva_absorb: The photos will all become publicly visible as part of the Feature's gallery. It will be sorted by photographic intent (Featured, Close Up, Context, General, Research, Undefined), then by date added.
blue_novel_owl_stab: Only the photos which have been assigned a photographic intent other than "undefined" will become publicly visible as part of the Feature's gallery. The rest will not appear anywhere.
happy_acidic_donkey_seek: Reworked images can be dropped into the viewer to replace the user-submitted image. The icon row in the bottom provides:
calm_away_panther_propel: The thumbnail gallery is a list of all images in the Feature's gallery. The photographic intent (e.g. Featured, Context, etc) can be clicked to set another intent.
last_front_toucan_type: Any good story needs a protagonist.
lucky_dark_larva_express: Back to superhero school with you.
warm_calm_badger_bask: Deleting this field also deletes the values on all features which use it. The data will be lost, and this action is irreversible once saved. Do you wish to continue?
active_nice_cougar_cook: If you remove yourself as owner or member, you will lose edit or access rights respectively
admin__forms_remove_self_warning: By removing yourself you'll no longer be able to manage this {resource}
admin__forms_hub_remove_organisation_war: By removing an organisation, you'll remove it and all its associated projects, layers and features from the hub
awful_loved_shrimp_cuddle: Content is shown in this language by default. If it's not unavailable, we might machine translate depending on your settings.
chunky_giant_worm_feast: If content is only available in your other language(s), we will show that instead of offering to machine translate it.
drab_lost_dove_grow: These settings control how and whether machine translation is used.
crazy_just_octopus_stop: automatically translates content to your preferred language. This means the full app experience will be in your preferred language, but the translations have not been validated by a human.
long_least_baboon_blend: gives you case-by-case control over whether machine-translation is used, and you can flip back and forth between the translation in your preferred language and the original text.
bland_honest_crossbill_amuse: shows placeholder text like "Unknown" or "Pending Translation" instead of machine-translating the content.
hub_organisations_empty_warning: If you remove all organisations for a hub, the map will be empty

import_batch_image_description: Drop images to automatically associate them with features based on filename
home_legal_trout_hurl: The toggles control the public visibility of the feature:
sea_born_mammoth_promise: ACCESSIBLE & OPEN TO PUBLIC — Feature is shown on the map if its layer is active. Set true if, in ordinary circumstances, the feature is accessible to the public without restrictions or permission. If the feature is temporarily inaccessible, this should be false.
nimble_tense_guppy_devour: INTANGIBLE FEATURE — Feature may be shown on the map with the caveat that there is (no longer) a physical presence on site. This invites visitors to experience the feature's practical, historic context — e.g. sites of historic events; a striking contrast with the present; or a locality that is more salient than the missing materiality of the feature.
orange_icy_slug_amaze: Note that if a feature is permanently unavailable AND there is no historic, intangible value to the site, it should be <u>unpublished</u> to unlist it from all maps.
weak_main_earthworm_thrive: Categorical dimensions along which the features can be filtered on the map. Fields are defined at the Project level.
less_new_crocodile_dust: Free-form text fields that provide additional context and references. Fields are defined at the Project level.

analytics__views_description: This facet is reserved for Cloudflare traffic and page analytics such as top hosts, paths, page-view trends, and referrer breakdowns.
analytics__audience_description: This facet is reserved for Cloudflare Web Analytics dimensions such as country, device, browser, operating system, and Core Web Vitals.
analytics__data_description: This facet is reserved for D1, Queue, and pipeline health metrics such as query latency, throughput, backlog, retries, and storage growth.
analytics__status_unwired: Cloudflare-backed data source is not wired for this facet yet.
analytics__scope_unimplemented: Prism-constrained analytics lookup has not been implemented yet.
analytics__views_next_step_1: Query Cloudflare zone or Web Analytics time series by hostname and path.
analytics__views_next_step_2: Add host and route filters that align with hub and project scope.
analytics__audience_next_step_1: Read country, browser, device, and performance series from Web Analytics.
analytics__audience_next_step_2: Break down audience by site hostname once production tokens are configured.
analytics__data_next_step_1: Join D1, R2, and Queue metrics into one operational data surface.
analytics__data_next_step_2: Expose backlog, latency, and error trends with fixed comparison windows.
analytics__tooltip_compare_windows: Compare the same analytics metrics across fixed rolling windows without changing the active prism scope.
analytics__empty_no_windows_metrics_desc: Select one or more windows to compare cache mix, latency, breakdowns, and leaders.
analytics__empty_no_windows_leaders_desc: Select one or more windows to compare the top transformations and assets.
analytics__empty_no_windows_failures_des: Select one or more windows to compare missing-image and 5xx failures.
analytics__leaders_tooltip: Top requested transformations and assets for the selected analytics windows.
analytics__failures_tooltip: Requested assets that failed for the selected analytics windows.
analytics__timeline_tooltip: Daily request mix for the last 30 days across the active prism scope.
analytics__timeline_empty_description: This view fills once Cloudflare has emitted daily asset-delivery data.
analytics__window_missing_description: This time range has not been emitted by the analytics engine yet.
analytics__latency_tooltip: Percentile response times for the active request classes in this time window.
analytics__transformations_tooltip: Request-weighted shares for crop mode, format, dimensions, quality, and gravity in this time window.
analytics__ranked_transformations_toolti: Top requested transformation signatures for this time window.
analytics__images_tooltip: Most requested public IDs in this time window.
analytics__missing_images_tooltip: Most requested public IDs that returned 404 in this time window.
analytics__server_error_assets_tooltip: Most requested public IDs that returned 5xx errors in this time window.
